channel.title: GiftScored
channel.description: Rubric-based gift scoring. Methodology published; weights on the page.

item.title: How we pick candidates
item.description: The candidate-selection pass: which sources bench a product, which disqualify one, and why commission rate is never an input.

item.title: How the score is calculated
item.description: The four weighted rubric dimensions, the failure modes each is designed to catch, and the sanity-check passes a Gift Score has to survive before publication.

item.title: Quarterly rubric refresh
item.description: Why the rubric gets re-audited every quarter, what moves between cycles, and how weight changes are logged so a reader can audit the drift.

item.title: Worked example: applying the rubric to anniversary gifts
item.description: Five candidate anniversary gifts scored dimension by dimension against the published rubric, with every sub-score and every composite written out in full.

item.title: Worked example: applying the rubric to graduation gifts
item.description: Five candidate graduation gifts run through the four rubric dimensions, with the arithmetic done in the open and the rationale for each sub-score on the page.

item.title: Worked example: applying the rubric to a mom's birthday
item.description: Five candidate birthday gifts for a parent scored against the rubric, with the weights doing visible separating work between shared-history objects and generic premium purchases.
